messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Agents window + AHP: Worktree failure error
ISSUE BODY: <!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
From https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/319764#issuecomment-4734066705, I'm now getting `Error: (sendFailed) Error: git worktree exited with code 128: fatal: Could not reset index file to revision 'HEAD'.` in the Agents window.

--- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) ---
--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# NOTE: This prompt is intended for internal use only for now.
agent: agent argument-hint: Provide a link or issue number to find duplicates for description: Find duplicates for a VS Code GitHub issue model: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (copilot) tools:
- execute/getTerminalOutput
- execute/runInTerminal
- github/*
- agent/runSubagent
---

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Agent host unit tests  
For tests in this area that touch the SessionDatabase, they MUST use an in-memory database, not a real database file on disk. Use `SessionDatabase.open(':memory:')` and see the examples from existing tests.  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\src\vs\platform\agentHost\test\auth-rework.md =====

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# ⚠️: Internal use only. To onboard, follow instructions at https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-engineering/blob/main/docs/gh-mcp-onboarding.md agent: agent model: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (copilot) argument-hint: Describe your issue. Include relevant keywords or phrases.
description: Search for an existing VS Code GitHub issue tools:
- github/*
- agent/runSubagent
---

--- File: openproject_full.md ---
### 4.3 File Services  
#### 4.3.1 GET Files (Header) Service  
_Not implemented_

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### Manual fallback  
If the launch skill cannot be used, the same scenario is achievable by hand: open Agents window, send the same prompts, inspect the chat UI, then `tail -f` the agentHost log under
`~/.config/Code - Insiders/logs/.../agentHost.log` (path varies by OS; `code-oss-logs` resolves it).

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Agents window + AHP: Worktree failure error",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Bug",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host-platform", "git-integration", "agents-window", "bug"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a VS Code user, I want the Agent Host Platform to properly handle git worktree operations without throwing fatal errors so that my AI agents can continue working in repositories with complex branch structures.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given a repository with multiple branches, When an agent attempts git worktree reset to HEAD, Then no 'fatal: Could not reset index file' error is thrown",
    "Given the git worktree operation fails due to index corruption, When retry logic is triggered, Then the operation should succeed on subsequent attempt or provide clear recovery guidance",
    "Given a user encounters this error in Agents window, When they view the agentHost log, Then it contains detailed diagnostic information about the failed operation",
    "Given the manual fallback path exists, When users cannot use launch skill, They can manually inspect agentHost logs to debug issues"
  ],
  "story_points": null,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Investigate root cause of git worktree exit code 128 in AHP implementation",
      "Review SessionDatabase and index file handling logic for potential corruption sources",
      "Implement retry mechanism with exponential backoff for failed git operations",
      "Add comprehensive error logging to agentHost.log for worktree failures"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Update Agents window error display to show user-friendly message when worktree fails",
      "Add link to manual fallback instructions in error dialog",
      "Improve error state handling in chat UI during failed operations"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Create unit tests for git worktree operation success scenarios",
      "Create integration tests simulating index file corruption",
      "Verify retry logic handles multiple failure modes correctly",
      "Validate agentHost log contains actionable diagnostic information"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Root cause identified and documented",
    "Retry mechanism implemented with proper backoff strategy",
    "Enhanced error logging in agentHost.log",
    "User-facing error messages updated",
    "All existing tests pass",
    "New tests added for worktree failure scenarios"
  ],
  "planning_status": "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What is the exact sequence of operations that triggers this git worktree error in AHP?",
    "Is this a regression from a previous version or a new scenario?",
    "Are there specific repository types or configurations that reproduce this issue more reliably?",
    "Has this been tested with different git versions and VS Code versions?",
    "What is the expected behavior when index file corruption occurs - should it retry, fail gracefully, or require manual intervention?",
    "Is there existing error handling code in agentHost that we need to extend?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The AHP (Agent Host Platform) handles git operations through some form of subprocess execution",
    "Index file corruption is the root cause rather than a network or permission issue",
    "The manual fallback path using tail -f agentHost.log is already functional"
  ],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent host unit tests documentation",
    "openproject_full.md - File Services reference (not directly relevant)",
    "GitHub issue #319764 - Original error report context"
  ]
}
```
